# docs/examples

Example DOT orchestration graphs. Demonstrates EpicRunner state machine implementing epic child tasks via AI agents. Runtime substitutes `EPIC_ID` placeholder, manages state files in `.pas/`, orchestrates `bd` CLI workflow (show, ready, update, close, sync).

## Contents

- [epic-runner.dot](./epic-runner.dot) ‚Äî EpicRunner digraph. Defines state machine nodes: `pick_task` (diamond, conditional, haiku model), `investigate` (box, bd tools), `implement` (box), `run_tests` (box), `verify` (diamond, conditional), `fixup` (box), `close_task` (box, git/bd tools), `check_remaining` (diamond, conditional, haiku model, loop_restart). Graph attributes: `goal="Implement all child tasks of epic EPIC_ID..."`, `model="sonnet"`. Tool restrictions: `Bash(bd:*)`, `Bash(git:*)`, `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob`.

## Behavioral Contracts

**Response Tokens** ‚Äî LLM outputs must terminate with exactly: `MORE`, `DONE`, `PASS`, `FAIL`.

**State Files** ‚Äî Runtime persists:
- `.pas/current_task.md` (active task ID, written by pick_task)
- `.pas/investigation.md` (codebase analysis, written by investigate)
- `.pas/test-results.md` (test output, written by run_tests)

**Placeholder** ‚Äî `EPIC_ID` string requires runtime substitution with actual epic identifier.

**Tool Restrictions** ‚Äî `Bash(bd:*)` (bd CLI commands) and `Bash(git:*)` (git operations) allowed only on `investigate`, `close_task` nodes. `Read`, `Grep`, `Glob` allowed on `investigate`.

**Edge Conditions** ‚Äî `preferred_label` attributes drive state transitions: `MORE` (pick_task‚Üíinvestigate), `DONE` (pick_task‚Üídone, check_remaining‚Üídone), `PASS` (verify‚Üíclose_task), `FAIL` (verify‚Üífixup).

## Reproduction-Critical Constants

Full prompt templates for all 8 nodes stored in annex file:
